episode_summary: Dan, James, Andy, and guest Angela Barnes share four new facts covering synesthesia and how some people perceive numbers with colors and personalities, strict royal etiquette around touching Elizabeth I and the role of the Master of the Horse, the quirks of Formula One licensing and driver ages, and a 16th-century chain of pubs serving purging ale. The episode mixes science, royal history, motorsport, and historical medicine with plenty of jokes and tangents.

facts.fact: OK, so my fact is that the number four is orange and a lot kinder, the number five, which has an attitude problem.
My fact is, in 1577, there was only one man in England permitted to touch the queen.
My fact this week is that the 10th best formula one driver in the world can't drive. He can't drive regular cars.
Okay, my fact this week is that in 16th century London, there was a chain of pubs that sold laxatives on tap.
